content.content:
1	You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
2	prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
3	out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
4	and open a Draft PR. Then stop.
5	
6	## Environment
7	
8	- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
9	  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
10	- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
11	- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
12	  branches; don't force-push.
13	- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
14	  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
15	  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
16	  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.
17	
18	## Workflow
19	
20	1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
21	   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
22	2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
23	   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
24	3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
25	   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
26	4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
27	   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
28	   plausible to run.
29	5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
30	   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
31	   Don't commit broken code.
32	6. Push and open PR (below).
33	
34	## Finishing
35	
36	```sh
37	git push -u origin HEAD
38	gh pr create --draft \
39	  --base main \
40	  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
41	  --body "<body>"
42	```
43	
44	If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
45	the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.
46	
47	**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:
48	
49	- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
50	- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
51	  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
52	- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
53	  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
54	  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
55	- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.
56	
57	After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.
58	
59	## Forbidden
60	
61	- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
62	- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
63	  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
64	  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
65	  ticket asks for it.
66	- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
67	- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
68	- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
69	  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
70	  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
71	  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.
72	
73	## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails
74	
75	Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
76	the blocker:
77	
78	1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
79	   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
80	   open the PR.
81	2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
82	   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
83	   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
84	   reviewer needs.
85	
86	Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
87	or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
88	beats a half-successful workaround.
89	
90	## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible
91	
92	Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
93	take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
94	explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.
95

file.content:
You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body "<body>"
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  ticket asks for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
   open the PR.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
   reviewer needs.

Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
beats a half-successful workaround.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

45	        with:

47	          # App installation token; needs Contents + Pull requests + Workflows
48	          # write so the agent can push branches that add or modify
49	          # .github/workflows/*.

51	
52	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4

86	        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
87	
88	      - name: Configure git user
89	        env:
90	          BOT_LOGIN: ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-login }}
91	          BOT_ID:    ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-id }}
92	        run: |
93	          git config --global user.name  "${BOT_LOGIN}"
94	          git config --global user.email "${BOT_ID}+${BOT_LOGIN}@users.noreply.github.com"
95

97	        env:
98	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}

102	          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main

121

135	            --model claude-opus-4-8 \
136	            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
137	            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
138	            --output-format stream-json \
139	            --verbose \
140	            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
141	            > stream.jsonl
142	          rc=$?
143	          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
144	          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
145	          exit 0
146	
147	      - name: Convert log to JSON array
148	        if: always()
149	        run: |
150	          set -euo pipefail
151	          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
152	            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
153	          else
154	            echo '[]' > execution.json
155	          fi
156	
157	      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
158	        if: always()
159	        env:
160	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
161	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
162	          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
163	        run: |
164	          set -euo pipefail
165	
166	          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
167	          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
168	            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
169	            exit 0
170	          fi
171	
172	          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
173	          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
174	          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
175	            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
176	            exit 0
177	          fi
178	
179	          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
180	          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true
181	
182	          gh pr create --draft \
183	            --base main \
184	            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
185	            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
186	            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."
187	
188	      - name: Commit execution log to main
189	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
190	        env:
191	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
192	        run: |
193	          set -e
194	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
195	          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
196	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
197	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
198	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
199	          cp execution.json "$DEST"
200	          git add "$DEST"
201	          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
202	          git push origin main
203	
204	      - name: Trigger agent improver
205	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
206	        env:
207	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
208	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
209	        run: |
210	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
211	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
212	            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
213	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
214	            -f log_ref=main

file.content:
name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '60'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - name: Generate App installation token
        id: app-token
        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
        with:
          app-id: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Resolve App bot identity for git commits
        id: app-user
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          APP_SLUG: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.app-slug }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          info=$(gh api "/users/${APP_SLUG}[bot]")
          echo "user-id=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .id)"       >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "user-login=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .login)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # App installation token; needs Contents + Pull requests + Workflows
          # write so the agent can push branches that add or modify
          # .github/workflows/*.
          token: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        env:
          BOT_LOGIN: ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-login }}
          BOT_ID:    ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-id }}
        run: |
          git config --global user.name  "${BOT_LOGIN}"
          git config --global user.email "${BOT_ID}+${BOT_LOGIN}@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --model claude-opus-4-8 \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.thinking:
Let me analyze what happened in this run:

1. The agent ran `jira issue view "KAN-33" --plain` to get the ticket — that worked fine.
2. It did a Glob search for README files — fine.
3. It read the main README.md — fine.
4. It made an edit to README.md — fine.
5. It read `.jira/config.yml` to get the JIRA_SERVER — fine.
6. It committed and pushed — fine.
7. It tried `gh pr create --draft --base main --title "..." --body "..."` with inline body text — **this failed** with: `Newline followed by # inside a quoted argument can hide arguments from path validation`.
8. The agent worked around this by writing the body to a temporary file and using `--body-file` instead, which succeeded, then cleaned up the temp file.

The core issue was that the harness's path validation rejected the `#` characters in the inline body argument, forcing a retry with a file-based approach.

The prompt currently shows the inline `--body` pattern, but it should demonstrate the `--body-file` approach to avoid this validation failure upfront. Looking at the tool allowlist, `Bash(gh pr create:*)` should permit any `gh pr create` command, so the block was coming from the harness's character validation rather than the tool allowlist itself — specifically the `#` character triggering the newline-in-quoted-argument check. I'm noticing the agent successfully ran `rm .pr-body.md` even though I don't see `Bash(rm:*)` explicitly in the allowlist, which suggests either the permission model auto-accepts certain cleanup commands or I'm missing something in how the allowlist is structured. Let me trace through the actual permissions to understand what was permitted. The agent ran into an issue where `gh pr create` with a multi-line body containing `#` headers got rejected by the path validator because newlines followed by `#` can obscure arguments. The agent worked around this by writing the body to a file and using `--body-file` instead. I should update the prompt example to use that approach rather than inline bodies. The cleanest approach is to have the agent write the PR body file using the Write tool first, then reference it with `--body-file` in the `gh pr create` command. This avoids the issues with Bash expansion of multi-line markdown and keeps the pattern consistent with how the prompt already handles config files.

content.text:
The key failure: `gh pr create --body "..."` with multi-line markdown containing `# headers` was rejected by the harness path validator (`Newline followed by # inside a quoted argument can hide arguments from path validation`). The agent had to discover `--body-file` on its own, costing one failed call plus a Write/rm workaround. The prompt's example still shows `--body "<body>"`.

The fix is to update the prompt's "Finishing" section to use `--body-file` from the start.

`--body "..."` with multi-line markdown (e.g. `## Summary`) is blocked by the
harness path validator. Then:

```sh
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body-file .pr-body.md
```

tool_use_result.oldString:

tool_use_result.originalFile:
You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body "<body>"
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  ticket asks for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
   open the PR.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
   reviewer needs.

Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
beats a half-successful workaround.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

input.command: gh pr create --base main --title "Prompt fix: junior-dev — use --body-file to avoid path validator block on gh pr create" --body "In run \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/2026-06-16/27617325248.json\`, the agent called \`gh pr create --body \"...\"\` with a multi-line markdown body containing \`## Summary\` headers. The harness path validator rejected it: \`Newline followed by # inside a quoted argument can hide arguments from path validation\`. The agent then had to discover the \`--body-file\` workaround itself (one failed call + Write + rm). This edit updates the prompt's Finishing section to direct the agent to write the body via the \`Write\` tool to \`.pr-body.md\` and pass \`--body-file\` to \`gh pr create\`, avoiding the failure entirely on future runs."

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/60

The run showed `gh pr create --body "..."` failing because the harness path validator rejects multi-line strings containing `#` characters. The prompt's Finishing section now tells the agent to write the body to `.pr-body.md` via `Write` and use `--body-file` instead, saving one failed call per run.
